promotions.behavior_rule: Do not store raw full Codex transcripts or message content in Wiki because the data repo is public.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Register sessions first as metadata-only local-only source archives plus a searchable Wiki registry.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Defer content-level extraction and accepted-memory promotion to a separate reviewed pass.
Do not push raw session archives or unreviewed extracted content to the public data repo.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Completion is defined as an evidence-backed OS loop, not a note vault or demo graph.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Current DinoBrain is documented as a strong foundation, not complete under the accepted conditions.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Completion requires live pre-response OS enforcement, fail-closed gates, closed task lifecycle, honest hybrid retrieval, bounded/index-backed candidate generation, graph health, lifecycle cleanup, source provenance, behavior eval, memory-use audit, feedback writeback, Observatory evidence, privacy/public-data safety, new-PC recovery equivalence, and release/sync hygiene.

In similar future work, apply this decision: A disagreement from reviewer 10 was incorporated by adding explicit bounded/index-backed sparse/dense candidate generation and cold operational data partition/compaction/archive requirements.
Use docs/OS_COMPLETION_CONDITIONS.md as the governing acceptance document before implementing the next DinoBrain OS changes.
Do not claim DinoBrain OS completion until the mandatory gates and proof registry are satisfied against live current data.
In similar future work, apply this decision: AGREE with the direction only if SQLite wiki and operations shards are the required live fast path, not optional fallback artifacts.
In similar future work, apply this decision: DISAGREE that scalability completion is met while wiki.sqlite, manifest.json, and dense-vectors.json are absent from the live data index state.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Completion requires bounded candidate selection and latency budgets; current verifiers measure elapsed time but do not fail on agreed performance thresholds.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Completion requires graph health freshness against the current accepted corpus and graph adjacency/index metadata, not a stale healthy record.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Completion requires cold data compaction/archive boundaries so raw sessions, unreviewed candidates, old tasks/traces/context packs, and broad accepted behavior rules do not dominate default retrieval.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Create or refresh live .dino/index/sqlite/wiki.sqlite and .dino/index/sqlite/manifest.json, then verify get_context_pack and wiki_search report candidate_source=sqlite_shards_v2 on the real data repo.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Define hot/warm/cold retention: raw sessions stay local-only and excluded, operational logs are partitioned/archived by time/project, and accepted behavior memories are type-budgeted so they do not swamp durable Wiki/Source/Project records.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Completion Reviewer 10 completed a read-only data architecture/performance review across dinobrain and dinobrain-data. Conclusion: AGREE with the intended index-first, SQLite-sharded, graph-aware, provenance-aware direction, but DISAGREE that the current system is scalability-complete. Current live data has operations.sqlite tracked/present, but no wiki.sqlite, no SQLite manifest, no dense-vectors index, and the persisted graph-health is stale/false healthy relative to the accepted corpus. Synthetic verifiers pass but report timings instead of enforcing latency budgets. Completion should require fresh wiki+operations SQLite shards, bounded indexed candidate selection, real BM25/dense retrieval constraints, graph adjacency freshness/health gates, cold-data archive/compaction policy, and explicit latency/freshness gates that fail when the prompt path regresses toward full-vault scans.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Observatory completion must be defined by visible operational truth, not only a passing graph UI fixture.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Graph-health freshness must be a gate; the current persisted graph-health file is stale relative to the wiki index and accepted corpus.

In similar future work, apply this decision: The live graph should prioritize semantic clusters, search, hover detail, and state visibility, and avoid dinosaur/fossil/decorative visual metaphors as completion criteria.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Active tasks must distinguish actually-running work from stale started records and show blocked/pending/verifier/main lanes explicitly.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Memory-use visibility must show provided, declared-used, observed-used, missing expected, hallucinated references, trust score, and graph-health snapshot, not just an audit count.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Regenerate graph health after wiki/operations index updates and fail readiness when graph-health is older than the index or inconsistent with accepted record count.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Add/verify an Observatory real-data health card that compares persisted graph-health to a fresh sampled health computation and flags stale false-healthy states.
Do not treat accepted behavior memories as source-truth citations by default; they prove prior agent/task claims, not external factual correctness.

In similar future work, apply this decision: All currently inspected accepted records are source_status=internal and resolve to .dino/traces/*; this is acceptable for agent behavior memory but insufficient for external facts or public claims.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Completion should require real 30_Sources/chunks/* records plus .dino/provenance/* links for factual claims, external evidence, citations, and last_verified metadata.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Graph/provenance health is currently stale or incomplete: graph-health reports zero accepted instances while wiki-index contains the accepted corpus, so completion requires regenerated health metrics and failing checks for missing source chunks/provenance.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Trust scoring should remain an audit of provided/declared/observed memory use, not a truth score.

In similar future work, apply this decision: LLM Wiki completion must require fast indexed candidate selection, honest retrieval-mode reporting, and true dense retrieval only when a usable dense vector index/provider exists.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Operational traces/accepted behavior rules currently create ranking pressure; completion requires durable wiki/source/project knowledge to outrank broad operational behavior rules when the query intent calls for it.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Graph completion is not the visual graph alone; it requires fresh indexed nodes/edges, wikilinks, provenance/lifecycle edges, graph-health freshness, and degraded status when empty or stale.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Quality completion is not satisfied until representative context and behavior golden sets pass under growth/noise budgets, including semantic paraphrase, rare exact queries, provenance, quarantine, and recency cases.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Latency completion should require the prompt path to avoid full vault scans and use SQLite wiki plus operations shards with measured query and rebuild budgets as data grows.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Create or refresh the wiki SQLite shard and manifest for the live data repo, then verify runtime candidate_source is sqlite_shards_v2 for wiki_search and get_context_pack.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Tune ranking and/or type-specific budgets so broad accepted behavior rules do not swamp durable wiki/source/project records.
In similar future work, apply this decision: User explicitly requested public visibility for clockmansy/dinobrain-data after clockmansy/dinobrain was made public.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Performed only a minimal tracked secret marker scan before changing visibility; this does not prove the repository contains no personal or operational memory content.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Used git credential-manager token in-process because gh CLI is not installed and the GitHub connector does not expose repository visibility update.
In similar future work, apply this decision: If the public data repository should be sanitized, run a deeper content review over accepted memories, traces, context packs, and events.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Only changed the app repository clockmansy/dinobrain; left dinobrain-data untouched because it contains operational memory/data and prior policy separates app release from data sync.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Used git credential-manager token in-process because gh CLI is not installed and the GitHub connector does not expose repository visibility update.
In similar future work, apply this decision: If the data repository should also become public, run a separate explicit review because it may contain user/session memory.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Changed GitHub repository visibility for clockmansy/dinobrain from private to public after verifying the target repository, admin permissions, and a minimal tracked-file secret scan. In similar future work, apply this decision: Treat this as an installer bug, not a path or quoting bug.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Normalize existing config text before section removal and block append.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Validate the final DinoBrain TOML block before and after writing, and reject bare CR bytes at the file level.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Bump patch version to 2.2.1 and tag it because this affects installer safety.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Fixed the installer Codex config writer so malformed carriage returns cannot corrupt <machine-local-path> Added CRLF normalization before TOML block rewriting, byte-level bare CR checks after write, DinoBrain TOML block shape validation, and a regression case that starts from a malformed config containing CRCRLF and bare CR. Bumped DinoBrain to v2.2.1 and pushed main/tag.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Version bumped to 2.2.0 for closed-loop compounding semantics.
In similar future work, apply this decision: finish_task runs runCompoundingCycle when DINOBRAIN_AUTO_COMPOUND=1, before auto_sync.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Installer and current Codex config/hook now set DINOBRAIN_AUTO_COMPOUND=1 along with auto growth and auto sync.
verify:os now fails if Codex/Claude hook or MCP env misses auto-compound wiring.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Knowledge compounding proof requires a promoted behavior rule to be retrieved by a later Context Pack and memory-on behavior lift to be positive.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Implemented DinoBrain OS v2.2.0 closed-loop compounding: finish_task can run automatic trace-to-behavior-rule distillation, cleanup invalid/duplicate behavior rules, refresh retrieval indexes, and keep auto sync enabled from installer/Codex hook config. Added verify:compounding to prove later memory search/context packs retrieve the promoted rule and memory-on behavior beats memory-off baseline.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Add auto_sync as a separate policy-enforced tool instead of changing git_sync dry-run semantics.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Enable auto growth and auto sync by installer/env configuration, while blocked raw conversation and secret-bearing paths remain excluded from automatic commits.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Move hook auto_sync after codex_preflight_completed event so the preflight completion event is included in the auto-sync commit.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Check GitHub Actions release run for tag v2.1.0 if release asset availability must be confirmed from GitHub UI.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Implemented DinoBrain OS v2.1.0 closed-loop automation: hard pre-response hook block metadata, hook-side auto sync after preflight completion, finish_task auto-growth records, policy-based auto_sync commit/push tool, search_memory alias, installer env wiring for auto growth/sync, current Codex config/hook env update, and flow audit verification showing all 8 user-flow steps verified.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Classify current DinoBrain as a compounding-capable foundation, not a fully autonomous compounding OS yet.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Define true compounding as repeated conversion of conversations into reviewed preferences, decisions, claims, corrections, and behavior rules that measurably improve future sessions.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Fix live version drift so the running MCP reports v2.0.2 after reload/restart.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Treat installed DinoBrain as configured and probe-verified, not proven fully live for the current active Codex turn until a fresh prompt after hook approval/reload produces codex_prompt_submitted and codex_preflight_completed without manual probe.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Report the 8-step loop as partially enforced: steps 2, 3, 5, 6 are directly verified; step 1 is configured and probe-verified; steps 4, 7, 8 have known automation/policy limits.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Start a fresh Codex thread after hook approval/reload and verify that codex_prompt_submitted and codex_preflight_completed appear before any manual MCP call.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Verified the installed/current DinoBrain OS loop against the user's 8-step flow. Codex MCP registration, UserPromptSubmit hook registration, hook simulation/probe, task/context pack creation, wiki_search, finish_task support, v2 verification, and git remotes are present. The complete loop is partially rather than fully autonomous: live current-thread hook injection was not observed before manual probe, model behavior cannot be runtime-enforced by DinoBrain, search_memory is not a separate tool, knowledge growth beyond accepted instances is not fully automatic, and git_sync remains dry-run/manual-review by policy.
In similar future work, apply this decision: App repo push authorized by user and completed on main.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Bumped DinoBrain to v2.0.2 because retrieval and gate semantics changed materially.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Dense retrieval now reports hybrid_contextual_v2 only when a usable dense vector index/provider exists for the query; otherwise it reports lexical_fallback_v2.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Kept candidate_source labels stable while bumping underlying wiki/sqlite index versions to v3.
In similar future work, apply this decision: os_gate no longer trusts has_context_pack/context_item_count self-report without a verified context pack trace.
In similar future work, apply this decision: audit_memory_use now separates declared artifacts from event-observed artifacts.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Review whether dinobrain-data index/task changes should be committed under the existing data-vault policy.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Released as v2.0.1 because this is a stability patch on top of v2.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Data repo runtime logs and indexes were not committed or pushed automatically.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Claude Code integration now writes a UserPromptSubmit hook in addition to MCP registration; official Claude Code docs define UserPromptSubmit as firing before Claude processes a prompt.
After installing v2.0.1 on another PC, restart/trust Codex and Claude hooks so active sessions reload the new pre-response hook config.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Implemented and deployed DinoBrain v2.0.1 stability fixes based on the QA review. Patched fail-closed Codex hook duplicate-lock behavior, added Claude Code UserPromptSubmit hook registration and verification, expanded v2 action gates to the full OS tool contract, made behavior eval strict when golden cases are missing, added source chunk redaction/truncation guards, made node lifecycle apply real archive/hold/merge/provenance repair operations, handled os_begin_task context-pack failures as fail-closed task/gate records, fixed Observatory JSON graph fallback for v2 indexes, bumped app/installer version to 2.0.1, added regression coverage, pushed main and tag v2.0.1. Local release ZIP was built; direct GitHub Release upload could not run because no GITHUB_TOKEN/GH_TOKEN is available in this shell.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Review remained read-only; no code changes were made.
In similar future work, apply this decision: v2 stability risk is mostly in fail-closed enforcement, automatic lifecycle execution, strict eval gating, and active-session MCP/tool reload guarantees.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Patch hook duplicate-lock path to inject fail-closed unless a concrete sibling preflight report/context can be read.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Completed stability-focused QA/code review of DinoBrain v2 changes. Findings: Codex hook duplicate-lock path can skip preflight instead of fail-closing; Claude Code integration is MCP-only and does not enforce pre-response OS context; action-gate required-tool checks still cover only legacy tools; node lifecycle apply=true writes review records rather than executing merge/hold/delete/provenance repair; behavior eval exits ok when golden file is missing; Observatory JSON graph fallback rejects v2 wiki index when SQLite is unavailable; create_source_chunk stores raw chunk_text without redaction/size bounds; os_begin_task can leave started tasks if context-pack construction throws. Type check passed after adding portable Node directory to PATH; eval:behavior currently returns ok/skipped because behavior-golden.json is missing.
Use os_begin_task as the default v2 pre-response entrypoint for hooks and agents.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Fail closed when OS context is missing or destructive-risk gates block the request.
Use local deterministic hybrid retrieval: contextual chunks, BM25 sparse, dense-lite lexical similarity, RRF, provenance and root-intent reranking.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Implement lifecycle actions as review records rather than silent destructive mutation.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Run installer build/release after review so other PCs install the v2 OS contract.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Run git_sync dry-run before syncing the data repo; do not auto-push data without review.
